title: HBP | Main | 1.3 • Route: Hilbert–Pólya Realizations via Blur
author: Perisic, Aleksandar
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
We give a compact route to RH organized around a blur-level positivity condition (BP2) for the calibrated phase measure on the critical line. BP2 produces a Herglotz limit and hence a canonical self-adjoint Hilbert-Pólya operator whose spectrum sits at the zero ordinates; the Gaussian blur also acts as a detector forbidding off-line mass. 
 A Helson prime-block mechanism supplies the model-side quantitative budgets Fit/Guard/Lin/Quad/Mom/Tail that place the prime/archimedean detector response in the polynomial regime on compact subsets of the upper half-plane. At each transition the raw stage increment is fresh-block by exact cancellation: the target $\zeta$-factor cancels from 
 $$ H_{j+1}-H_j=\log \left(\zeta_{\chi_{j+1}} / \zeta_{\chi_j}\right) $$ 
 and all frozen old Euler factors cancel as well. What remains after the four-flow and guard reduction is the detector-side sign-cancellation issue, more precisely the detector-localized regulated sign-loss channel arising after localization to the relevant detector window, Poisson lifting, and Gaussian scanning. 
 A dedicated discussion explains why a Helson-only route does not close RH and why the blur/Herglotz/spectral steps are essential. We emphasize the method's mechanical simplicity: all moving parts are explicit, modular, and checkable, and the chain is tied together by a single positivity condition, BP2, once the detector-localized sign-loss obstruction has been discharged.
